2603.16350.title: Explainable machine learning workflows for radio astronomical data processing
2603.16350.paper_summary: Radio astronomy relies heavily on efficient and accurate processing pipelines to deliver science ready data. With the increasing data flow of modern radio telescopes, manual configuration of such data processing pipelines is infeasible. Machine learning (ML) is already emerging as a viable solution for automating data processing pipelines. However, almost all existing ML enabled pipelines are of black-box type, where the decisions made by the automating agents are not easily deciphered by astronomers. In order to improve the explainability of the ML aided data processing pipelines in radio astronomy, we propose the joint use of fuzzy rule based inference and deep learning. We consider one application in radio astronomy, i.e., calibration, to showcase the proposed approach of ML aided decision making using a Takagi-Sugeno-Kang (TSK) fuzzy system. We provide results based on simulations to illustrate the increased explainability of the proposed approach, not compromising on the quality or accuracy.
2603.16350.paper_summary_zh: 無線天文學在高效且準確的處理管道上依賴甚重，以提供科學準備好的數據。隨著現代無線望遠鏡數據流的增加，手動配置這些數據處理管道已變得不可行。機器學習（ML）已經成為自動化數據處理管道的可行解決方案。然而，幾乎所有現有的 ML 啟用管道都是黑箱型的，自動化代理所做的決策對天文學家來說並不容易解讀。為了提高無線天文學中 ML 輔助數據處理管道的可解釋性，我們提出聯合使用模糊規則推理和深度學習。我們考慮無線天文學中的一個應用，即校準，以展示使用 Takagi-Sugeno-Kang（TSK）模糊系統的 ML 輔助決策方法。我們提供基於模擬的結果，以說明所提方法的可解釋性有所增加，且不妥協於質量或準確性。
2603.16350.author: S. Yatawatta et.al.
2603.16350.authors: S. Yatawatta, A. Ahmadi, B. Asabere, M. Iacobelli, N. Peters, M. Veldhuis
